chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH AT KARACHI Cr. Transfer Application No.41 of 2025 DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATUREs OF JUDGEs For orders on office objection For hearing of main case --------------------------------------------- 21.05.2025 Mr. Wahid Bux Chohan, advocate for applicant Mr. Qamaruddin Nohri, D.P.G. Respondent No.1 Fahah Shaikh & Respondent No.5 Faiza Shaikh in person ASI Mumtaz Ali of PS Ferozabad ------------------------------------ Applicant/complainant seeks transfer of Sessions Case No.3211/2023, arising out of FIR No.807/2022, registered at P.S. Ferozabad, for offence under sections 109, 324, 34, PPC from the file of learned IV Additional Sessions Judge Karachi East to any other competent Court of law. Pursuant to Court notice, respondents 1 and 5 appeared and submit that the remaining respondents are their brothers and sister and they have recorded no objection for transfer of aforesaid sessions case from the file of learned IV Additional Sessions Judge Karachi East to any other competent Court of law. Learned D.P.G. has also recorded no objection. By consent, instant transfer application is allowed and the aforesaid sessions case is transferred from the file of learned IV Additional Sessions Judge Karachi East to learned Sessions Judge, Karachi East, who may proceed with the case himself or assign the same to any other Court for disposal in accordance with law. However, learned trial Court is directed to conclude the trial preferably within a period of three months, without granting adjournment to either party on any flimsy ground, under intimation to this Court through learned MIT-II.

By consent, instant transfer application is allowed and the aforesaid sessions case is transferred from the file of learned IV Additional Sessions Judge Karachi East to learned Sessions Judge, Karachi East, who may proceed with the case himself or assign the same to any other Court for disposal in accordance with law. However, learned trial Court is directed to conclude the trial preferably within a period of three months, without granting adjournment to either party on any flimsy ground, under intimation to this Court through learned MIT-II. J U D G E Gulsher/PS
